elements.sent-2411-009: A priori bounds serve as a crucial first step in proving renormalization convergence by providing a uniform control over the size of the iterates of the map across renormalization steps.

elements.sent-2411-014: In this series of papers, we set the stage for theoretical non-perturbative a priori bounds machinery for real two-dimensional H\'enon-like maps.
elements.sent-2411-015: The work begins with \cite{CLPY1 , where we introduce a class of regularly unicritical, infinitely renormalizable diffeomorphisms.}
elements.sent-2411-016: For maps in this class, we show that the return maps to renormalization domains are regular H\'enon-like, meaning they have topological 2D horsehoe shapes that converge super-exponentially fast to 1D parabolic shapes.
elements.sent-2411-017: This property is the entrance point to the current paper, where we establish crucial a priori bounds for this class of maps that provide us with the bounded distortion and uniform differentiable shapes of all the renormalizations.
elements.sent-2411-018: In the forthcoming paper (\cite{Y ), these results will be used for establishing renormalization convergence and universality in the class of H\'enon-like maps.}

elements.sent-2411-568: In this section, we summarize some of the techniques in 1D dynamical systems used to control distortion.
elements.sent-2411-570: Let $f : I \to f(I)$ be a $C^1$-diffeomorphism on an interval $I \subset \bbR$.
elements.sent-2411-571: For $J \subset I$, the {\it distortion of $f$ on $J$} is defined as \begin{equation}\Dis(f, J) := \sup_{x, y \in J}\frac{|f'(x)|}{|f'(y)|}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-573: For $K \geq 1$, we say that $f$ has {\it $K$-bounded distortion on $J$} if \begin{equation}\Dis(f, J) \leq K.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-574: Clearly, if $g : I' \to g(I')$ is another $C^1$-diffeomorphism on an interval $I' \supset f(J)$, then we have \begin{equation}\label{eq.dis product} \Dis(g \circ f, J) \leq \Dis(g, f(J)) \cdot \Dis(f, J).\end{equation}.
